title: Tickmill Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)
meta_title: Tickmill Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026) | TradingToolsHub
meta_description: Tickmill Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). Tickmill (4.2★, Free). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using Tickmill — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is Tickmill?

Tickmill is a London-based ECN/STP forex and CFD broker regulated by the FCA, CySEC, DFSA, and FSA, serving active traders since 2014. The platform offers raw spreads starting from 0.0 pips, order execution averaging under 0.20 seconds, and support for both MT4 and MT5 trading terminals across 80+ instruments including forex, indices, commodities, bonds, and crypto. With a 4.2/5 rating and zero monthly account fees across all four tiers, Tickmill targets active forex scalpers, algorithmic traders, and professionals seeking transparent pricing over market spreads.

How to Create Your Tickmill Account

Step 1: Visit the Tickmill Website and Select Account Type

  • Navigate to the Tickmill signup page and choose between Demo, Classic, Pro, or VIP accounts. Demo accounts require no deposit and grant access to the full platform with virtual funds for 30 days. Live accounts require a minimum of $100 for Classic and Pro, or $50,000 for VIP access to premium features.

Step 2: Enter Your Personal Information

  • Provide your full name, email address, country of residence, and phone number. Tickmill uses this information to verify your identity and determine which regulatory regime applies to your account.

Step 3: Complete KYC Verification

  • Upload a government-issued ID (passport or driver's license) and proof of address (utility bill or bank statement dated within the last 90 days). Verification typically completes within 1–2 business hours during UK business hours.

Step 4: Answer Your Trading Profile Questions

  • Complete a questionnaire about your trading experience, annual income, investment knowledge, and risk tolerance. This determines whether you qualify for retail, professional, or eligible counterparty classification under FCA rules.

Step 5: Accept Terms and Fund Your Account

  • Review the terms of service and risk disclaimers, then check the box to proceed. For live accounts, you'll be asked to fund your account via bank transfer, credit/debit card, or e-wallets like Skrill and Neteller. Deposits process within 1–3 business days depending on your payment method.

Estimated Setup Time: 15–20 minutes for account creation, plus 1–2 hours for KYC approval if you submit documents during business hours.

Setting Up Tickmill for the First Time

Downloading and Logging Into MT4/MT5

After your account is approved, download MT4 or MT5 from the Tickmill download page. Open the platform, select "File" → "Login to Trading Account," and enter your account number, password, and select the correct server (Tickmill-Demo or Tickmill-Live depending on your account type). The platform will sync with Tickmill's servers within seconds.

Configuring Your Dashboard Layout

MT4 and MT5 arrive with a default single-chart layout. Customize this by opening multiple chart windows (File → New Chart) and arranging them side-by-side or in tabs. Add your primary pairs (EURUSD, GBPUSD) plus your target markets (indices like GER30, commodities like Gold, or crypto like BTC/USD). Save your workspace layout as a template via File → Save Template.

Setting Up Alerts and Order Templates

Use the Alerts feature (Tools → Alerts) to notify yourself when key price levels are hit or volatility spikes. Tickmill's platform includes a news feed integrated into the terminal; enable it via View → Toolbox → News. For automated trading, create order templates under Tools → Options → Expert Advisors to pre-configure lot size, slippage tolerance, and risk parameters.

Enabling API Access (VIP Accounts Only)

VIP traders can request FIX API credentials from Tickmill support. FIX API allows direct integration with third-party trading systems and backtesting frameworks. This requires additional verification and is best handled by contacting the VIP onboarding team directly.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Raw Spreads from 0.0 Pips (Pro and VIP Accounts)

Pro and VIP accounts display the interbank spread directly—often starting at 0.0 pips on major pairs—plus a fixed commission (typically $2.50–$3.50 per 100k traded). This transparent model means you pay what you see rather than guessing at hidden markup. Compare this against brokers like Interactive Brokers, which bundle spreads and commissions; Tickmill's model favors high-frequency scalpers.

2. Backtesting and Strategy Optimization

MT5 includes a built-in Strategy Tester accessible via View → Toolbox → Strategy Tester. Upload your Expert Advisor, select historical price data (1-minute to daily), and run backtests on any date range in Tickmill's 20+ year history. Use Monte Carlo analysis to test robustness across different market regimes. The tester is free for all account types.

3. Paper Trading / Demo Mode

Demo accounts are fully live in feature set—same spreads and execution as your live account, but funded with virtual money. Use this to test new strategies, learn the platform, or verify your automated trading setup works before risking real capital. Tickmill refreshes demo accounts with new balance every 30 days.

4. Mobile App with Full Order Management

The MT4 and MT5 mobile apps (iOS and Android) allow you to open/close trades, set alerts, and monitor the news feed on the go. Orders execute with the same sub-0.20-second latency as the desktop platform. This is essential for swing traders who cannot watch charts all day.

5. Expert Advisors and Algorithmic Trading

All account types support Expert Advisors (EAs) directly in MT4 and MT5. Write in MQL4/MQL5, upload your EA, and it will run on Tickmill's servers 24/5 with VPS optional support. VIP clients additionally get FIX API access for more sophisticated algorithmic systems. No restrictions on high-frequency or scalping strategies.

6. Custom Indicators and Third-Party Tools

The MT4/MT5 marketplace hosts hundreds of custom indicators. Install them by dragging .ex4/.ex5 files into your Experts folder, then apply them to any chart. Tickmill allows custom indicators on all accounts without licensing fees.

7. Risk Management Tools

Built into MT4/MT5: guaranteed stop-losses (prevents slippage on major announcements), take-profit orders, and position sizing based on account equity. Use the "Position Size Calculator" in most trading terminals to auto-compute lot size given your risk percentage and stop distance.

Tickmill Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Demo Account – $0/month

Ideal for: Complete beginners, strategy testers, platform explorers. Comes with $10,000 virtual balance, resets every 30 days. No credit card required. Use this for at least 2–4 weeks before opening a live account.

Classic Account – $0/month, $100 minimum deposit

Spreads: Fixed 1.5 pips on EURUSD (no commission). Entry-level for retail traders. If you trade fewer than 10 round-trips per month or prefer stable, predictable costs, this tier minimizes surprises. Not ideal for scalpers—the fixed spread wipes out profit on sub-10-pip moves.

Pro Account – $0/month, $100 minimum deposit

Spreads: Raw 0.0 pips + $2.50–$3.50 per 100k commission. Best for active day traders and scalpers who take 20+ trades daily. At 50 trades/day, you'll save $150–$200 monthly compared to Classic's fixed spreads. This is the most popular tier for professional retail traders.

VIP Account – $0/month, $50,000 minimum deposit

Spreads: Raw 0.0 pips + $1.50–$2.50 per 100k commission (lower than Pro). Includes FIX API, dedicated account manager, and priority support. Only worthwhile if you trade 5+ figures monthly and can justify the $50k lock-up. Consider this tier if you're running a prop trading or hedge fund operation.

Recommendation by Trader Type: Beginners start with Demo. Casual traders ($500–$5,000 accounts) use Classic to avoid commission surprises. Day traders with accounts $10k+ use Pro. Institutions use VIP for API access and negotiated commissions.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of Tickmill

1. Use Tickmill's Raw Spreads to Trade News and Volatility

The 0.0 pip spreads on Pro/VIP apply only when the market is liquid (typically 08:00–17:00 London time). Outside these hours, spreads widen to 2–5 pips. Schedule your high-frequency or scalping sessions during London and US market overlap (13:00–17:00 GMT) to capture the tightest spreads. Avoid trading pairs like USDCAD during Asian hours unless you're willing to accept 3–5 pip slippage.

2. Backtest with Tickmill's Actual Spread Data

When you backtest in MT5's Strategy Tester, set the "Spread" parameter to match your account type (1.5 for Classic, 0.0+commission for Pro). Many traders backtest with 0-spread assumptions and get shocked by slippage when live. Tickmill's tester uses real historical spreads—use them.

3. Combine Tickmill with ThinkOrSwim or TradingView for Charting**

While MT4/MT5 are powerful, traders often use external charting platforms for superior analysis. Connect your Tickmill account to Interactive Brokers' TWS or TradingView's broker integration, then execute orders through Tickmill. This gives you best-in-class charting plus Tickmill's tight spreads—the best of both worlds.

4. Enable Alerts Instead of Watching Charts All Day

Set price alerts at key support/resistance levels (File → Open Alert in MT4), and let the platform notify you when prices approach entry zones. This reduces screen time fatigue and lets you focus on analyzing trade setups rather than staring at moving candlesticks.

5. Test Your Expert Advisors on a VPS Before Live Deployment

If running an EA, rent a VPS (DigitalOcean, AWS, or Tickmill's partner VPS) and run your EA there instead of your home computer. A VPS ensures your EA executes 24/5 without interruptions from internet drops or power outages. Cost is ~$5–$10/month and eliminates missed trades.

6. Leverage the News Feed for Economic Calendar Events

Tickmill's integrated news feed in MT4/MT5 displays the economic calendar and major announcements. Set alerts 30 minutes before high-impact events (Fed announcements, jobs reports, GDP) so you can decide whether to close positions, widen stops, or sit out. Spreads typically triple during these windows.

7. Use Guaranteed Stop-Losses Before Major Events

Enable guaranteed stop-losses (a Tickmill feature) on your key positions before economic announcements or central bank meetings. This locks in your max loss even if the market gaps past your stop. The small fee (~1–2 pips) is insurance against catastrophic slippage.

Common Tickmill Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: Verification Takes Longer Than 2 Hours

If your KYC verification doesn't complete within 2 hours, it's likely submitted outside UK business hours (weekends/evenings) or the photo quality was poor. Solution: Re-submit during UK business hours (09:00–17:00 Monday–Friday, GMT) with high-resolution, well-lit photos of both sides of your ID and address proof.

Issue 2: Expert Advisors Won't Start on Tickmill Servers

Common cause: The EA was compiled for a different MT4 build than Tickmill's, or the EA has been disabled in your platform settings. Solution: Go to Tools → Options → Expert Advisors and ensure "Allow live trading" and "Allow algorithmic trading" are both checked. Recompile your EA in the same MT4/MT5 version Tickmill uses (typically the latest).

Issue 3: Spreads Are Wider Than Advertised

Advertised spreads (0.0 pips) apply during peak London/US hours. Outside 08:00–17:00 GMT, spreads widen to 2–5 pips during Asian and early European sessions. Solution: Schedule your trading for peak hours, or accept wider spreads if you trade after-hours. Check Tickmill's spread statistics page to see real-time spreads before you trade.

Issue 4: Deposits or Withdrawals Take Too Long

Bank transfers typically take 1–3 business days depending on your bank and destination country. E-wallets (Skrill, Neteller) process within 1–2 hours. Solution: Plan your deposits 3–5 business days before you plan to trade. If you need faster access, use an e-wallet despite the 2–3% fee.

Is Tickmill Worth It? Our Verdict

Tickmill earns a 4.2/5 rating and is worth opening an account if you scalp forex, trade indices, or run automated strategies. The $100 minimum, zero account fees, and 0.0 pip raw spreads on Pro/VIP accounts make it cost-competitive against competitors like Pepperstone and XM. However, the high $50,000 VIP minimum and lack of proprietary tools mean intermediate traders might prefer IG's leverage or Interactive Brokers' ecosystem. Start with a Demo account for 2 weeks, then upgrade to Pro if the execution speed and spreads fit your strategy.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
